items.prompt: Cloud virtual machines help collaborative reproducibility mainly by:
choices: Providing a consistent runtime environment across different users
Removing the need for any dataset versioning
Automatically converting restricted health licenses to public domain
items.explain: Example 13.47 provisions cloud VMs so collaborators share a consistent workflow environment.

items.prompt: Environment drift can cause identical code to fail or produce different outputs on another machine because of dependency or environment differences.
items.explain: Example 13.50 describes environment drift as a common reproducibility failure mode.

items.prompt: In Example 13.55, DVC audit trails primarily link:
choices: Retraining runs to the corresponding data versions and model parameters
Only slide titles to quiz item IDs
Only cloud invoices to lecture durations
Only FAIR Findable keywords to CAP partitions
items.explain: Example 13.55 tracks new datasets and updated model parameters whenever the model is retrained.

items.prompt: Name one reason climate-modeling collaborations such as CMIP6 need strong reproducibility practices.
items.rubric: Accept large/petabyte data, long simulations, multi-institution comparison, independent verification, or policy-facing transparency.
items.sample_answer: Large multi-institution datasets and long simulations must be independently verifiable for transparent policy use.

items.prompt: Containers or virtual environments such as conda are presented as mitigations for environment drift.
items.explain: Example 13.50 points to Docker and conda-style environments to keep runs consistent across machines.
